steps: Start with \(b = 42cf\). To solve for \(c\), divide both sides by \(42f\): \(c = \frac{b}{42f}\).
Both \(42\) and \(f\) are multiplied by \(c\) in the original equation, so both belong in the denominator when isolating \(c\).

• A \(c = \frac{b}{42f}\): divides \(b\) by \(42f\) ✓. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B \(c = \frac{b - 42}{f}\): subtracts 42 instead of dividing by it. Eliminated.
  • C \(c = 42bf\): multiplies instead of divides. Eliminated.
  • D \(c = 42 - b - f\): treats the equation as subtraction rather than multiplication. Eliminated.
